title: Beam Crossing
steps: Mice are placed at the beginning of an elevated horizontal plexiglass beambar covered on scotch tape to avoid limb slip (1m length – 2.5cm width- 0.5melevation). At the beginning of the beam we place a little lamp and at the endof the beam we place a dark box to motivate the animal to go towards the darkside.
Mice are allowed to explore the beam bar and receive four assisted trials tocross it a week before the test. This is called the "training period"
Clean the beam bar between animals.
For each video, the time it takes the animal to cross the beam is recorded, then the average time of the three trials is taken in BORIS Software (DOI: 10.1111/2041-210X.12584 ) for analysis.
The limb errors per video are also counted. An error is counted when, during a forward movement, alimb (forelimb or hindlimb) slips through the beam. The average number of foot slips is then taken.

The week after training, the beam crossing test starts and animals can traverse the entire length of the beam unassisted for three times. Each of the three trials are filmed for later analysis in BORIS software.
creator: Chelsie Steele
